quotes.text: Bishop Pontoppidan's 1752 'Natural History of Norway' described the kraken as the size of a small island.
Hans Egede, missionary to Greenland, sketched the great sea serpent he saw in 1734 — a hundred-foot beast.
Olaus Magnus's 1539 Carta Marina filled every blank stretch of ocean with a teeth-bearing leviathan.
Renard's 1717 'Fishes of Amboina' included an illustrated mermaid — fully classified, like a tuna.
Pliny the Elder swore sirens existed; medieval bestiaries kept his entry on the books for 1,500 years.
Job 41 describes Leviathan: 'his sneezings flash forth light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.'
Orkney fishermen swore a wife who walked the shore at low tide had hidden her seal-skin in the rafters.
Maps of Africa drew Blemmyae — men with no heads and faces in their chests — well into the 1600s.
